title: Open Strings — Names and Sound
description: Play each open string in turn, saying the note name out loud as you pluck. The goal is to connect the physical string to its name and sound.
instructions: Pluck the E string (lowest, thickest) on beat 1. Say 'E' out loud as you play it.
Let it ring for 4 beats, then pluck the A string. Say 'A'.
Continue to D, then G, saying each name out loud.
Repeat until you can name each string immediately without hesitating.

tab.label: Low to high, one per measure

variations.label: Harder: name the string before you pluck it

tab.label: Random order — point to a string, name it, then play

ensembleNotes: Knowing your string names instantly is table stakes for communicating with other musicians. When a guitarist says 'hit the G on beat 1', you need to know immediately which string and where without thinking.
